From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Territorial subdivisions of France

The cities of [Lyon](/wiki/Lyon "Lyon"), [Marseille](/wiki/Marseille "Marseille"), and [Paris](/wiki/Arrondissements_of_Paris "Arrondissements of Paris") are also divided into [municipal arrondissements](/wiki/Municipal_arrondissements_of_France "Municipal arrondissements of France"), not to be confused with the arrondissements addressed in this article.

| * [v](/wiki/Template:Administrative_divisions_of_France "Template:Administrative divisions of France") * [t](/wiki/Template_talk:Administrative_divisions_of_France "Template talk:Administrative divisions of France") * [e](/wiki/Special:EditPage/Template:Administrative_divisions_of_France "Special:EditPage/Template:Administrative divisions of France") |

An ***arrondissement*** (English: [/əˈrɒndɪsmənt/](/wiki/Help:IPA/English "Help:IPA/English"), French: [[aʁɔ̃dismɑ̃]](/wiki/Help:IPA/French "Help:IPA/French") [ⓘ](/wiki/File:LL-Q150_(fra)-0x010C-arrondissement.wav "File:LL-Q150 (fra)-0x010C-arrondissement.wav"))[[1]](#cite_note-1) is the third level of [administrative division in France](/wiki/Administrative_divisions_of_France "Administrative divisions of France") generally corresponding to the territory overseen by a [subprefect](/wiki/Subprefect "Subprefect"). As of 2023, the 101 French [departments](/wiki/Departments_of_France "Departments of France") are divided into 333 arrondissements (including 13 overseas).[[2]](#cite_note-2)

The capital of an arrondissement is called a [subprefecture](/wiki/Subprefectures_in_France "Subprefectures in France"). When an arrondissement contains the [prefecture](/wiki/Prefectures_in_France "Prefectures in France") (capital) of the department, that prefecture is the capital of the arrondissement, acting both as a prefecture and as a subprefecture. Arrondissements are further divided into [communes](/wiki/Communes_of_France "Communes of France").

The term [arrondissement](/wiki/Arrondissement "Arrondissement") can be roughly translated into English as district.[[3]](#cite_note-3)

[Some municipalities](/wiki/Administrative_divisions_of_Quebec#Local_municipalities "Administrative divisions of Quebec") in [Quebec](/wiki/Quebec "Quebec") are divided into arrondissements, reflecting the province’s historical link to [New France](/wiki/New_France "New France").

## Role and administration

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Arrondissements_of_France&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Role and administration")]

The administration of an arrondissement is assigned to a subprefect ([French](/wiki/French_language "French language"): *sous-préfet*) who assists the departmental prefect (*préfet*).

Unlike French [regions](/wiki/Regions_of_France "Regions of France"), departments and communes, arrondissements do not have the status of [legal entity](/wiki/Juristic_person "Juristic person") in [public law](/wiki/Public_law "Public law"). In addition, unlike those other administrative divisions, they are not run by elected officials, but by political appointees, officials appointed by the [French president](/wiki/President_of_the_French_Republic "President of the French Republic").

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Arrondissements_of_France&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: History")]

The concept of arrondissements was proposed several times as an administrative reform during the *[Ancien Régime](/wiki/Ancien_R%C3%A9gime "Ancien Régime")*, notably by the *[intendant](/wiki/Intendant "Intendant")* of the [généralité](/wiki/G%C3%A9n%C3%A9ralit%C3%A9 "Généralité") of [Brittany](/wiki/Brittany "Brittany"), [Caze de La Bove](/w/index.php?title=Gaspard_Louis_Caze_de_La_Bove&action=edit&redlink=1 "Gaspard Louis Caze de La Bove (page does not exist)"), in his *Mémoire concernant les subdélégués de l'intendance de Bretagne* in 1775.

The arrondissements were created after the [French Revolution](/wiki/French_Revolution "French Revolution") by the *Loi du 28 [pluviôse](/wiki/Pluvi%C3%B4se "Pluviôse")* in the year VIII of the [Republican Calendar](/wiki/French_Republican_Calendar "French Republican Calendar") (17 February 1800) and replaced "districts". In certain periods in French history, they have served a role in legislative elections, especially during the [Third Republic](/wiki/French_Third_Republic "French Third Republic"). In 1926, 106 arrondissements were suppressed by the government.[[4]](#cite_note-4)[[5]](#cite_note-5) While it claimed it was to achieve fiscal savings, some political analysts considered the results electoral manipulation. Some of these suppressed arrondissements were restored in 1942.

### Changes

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Arrondissements_of_France&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Changes")]

The most recent creations and disestablishments of arrondissements are listed in the table below.

| Year | Department | Created arrondissement(s) | Disbanded arrondissement(s) |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 2015 | [Moselle](/wiki/Moselle_(department) "Moselle (department)") | [Forbach-Boulay-Moselle](/wiki/Arrondissement_of_Forbach-Boulay-Moselle "Arrondissement of Forbach-Boulay-Moselle") | [Forbach](/wiki/Arrondissement_of_Forbach "Arrondissement of Forbach"), [Boulay-Moselle](/wiki/Arrondissement_of_Boulay-Moselle "Arrondissement of Boulay-Moselle") |
| 2015 | [Moselle](/wiki/Moselle_(department) "Moselle (department)") | [Metz](/wiki/Arrondissement_of_Metz "Arrondissement of Metz") | [Metz-Campagne](/wiki/Arrondissement_of_Metz-Campagne "Arrondissement of Metz-Campagne"), [Metz-Ville](/wiki/Arrondissement_of_Metz-Ville "Arrondissement of Metz-Ville") |
| 2015 | [Moselle](/wiki/Moselle_(department) "Moselle (department)") | [Sarrebourg-Château-Salins](/wiki/Arrondissement_of_Sarrebourg-Ch%C3%A2teau-Salins "Arrondissement of Sarrebourg-Château-Salins") | [Château-Salins](/wiki/Arrondissement_of_Ch%C3%A2teau-Salins "Arrondissement of Château-Salins"), [Sarrebourg](/wiki/Arrondissement_of_Sarrebourg "Arrondissement of Sarrebourg") |
| 2015 | [Moselle](/wiki/Moselle_(department) "Moselle (department)") | [Thionville](/wiki/Arrondissement_of_Thionville "Arrondissement of Thionville") | [Thionville-Est](/wiki/Arrondissement_of_Thionville-Est "Arrondissement of Thionville-Est"), [Thionville-Ouest](/wiki/Arrondissement_of_Thionville-Ouest "Arrondissement of Thionville-Ouest") |

## Statistics

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Arrondissements_of_France&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Statistics")]

Most departments have three or four arrondissements. The departments of [Paris](/wiki/Arrondissement_of_Paris "Arrondissement of Paris") and of the [Territoire de Belfort](/wiki/Territoire_de_Belfort "Territoire de Belfort") have only one, while the department of [Pas-de-Calais](/wiki/Pas-de-Calais "Pas-de-Calais") has seven. [Mayotte](/wiki/Mayotte "Mayotte") has none.
